basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Anfāl — "The Spoils of War" — is a Medinan sūra born of the battle of Badr, that "Day of Discernment" (yawm al-furqān) on which God parted the true from the false by the edge of an improbable victory. Its first sentence — "They question you about the spoils. Say: the spoils belong to God and the Messenger" — at once overturns the perspective: what seems a battle's plunder is, in reality, a grace whose destination God alone appoints. The sūra advances in concentric circles: the spoils, the battle, the pact, fidelity, the union of hearts. Qushayrī deciphers in it the great Sufi law of the real Agent: "it was not you who slew them, but God slew them; you did not throw when you threw, but God threw" — separation is the attribute of servanthood, union the attribute of Lordship, and the servant stands upright only between the two. At verse 24 comes the most overwhelming word of the sūra: "And know that God comes between a man and his heart" — so that he may have no recourse but to Him. And throughout, the sūra unfolds the unity of the outer combat and the inner jihād: in the greater jihād, too, there is a spoil — that the servant retake possession of his soul, which was in the hands of the enemy: passion and the devil.
themes: the spoils belong to God and the Messenger
Badr and the Day of Discernment
God between a man and his heart
fidelity of the pact, union of hearts

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْأَنفَالِ ۖ قُلِ الْأَنفَالُ لِلَّهِ وَالرَّسُولِ ۖ فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَأَصْلِحُوا ذَاتَ بَيْنِكُمْ ۖ وَأَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: yasʾalūnaka ʿani l-anfāl, quli l-anfālu li-llāhi wa-r-rasūl, fa-ttaqū llāha wa-aṣliḥū dhāta baynikum, wa-aṭīʿū llāha wa-rasūlahu in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: They question you about the spoils. Say: "The spoils belong to God and the Messenger. So fear God, set right the bond between you, and obey God and His Messenger if you are believers."
versets.commentaire: The sūra opens upon a question — to whom does the booty belong? — and the answer overturns the perspective: it belongs first to God and His Messenger, and falls to men only by decree. Qushayrī: respond to the command of God and do not obey the callings of your desires; seek to prefer the contentment of the Real to the will of the lower self. And the concord is possible only by tearing oneself from the avarice of the soul, preferring the right of another to one's own share, and purifying the hearts of the hidden folds of envy and rancour.

mots.def: to ask, to pose a question.
nafl: what is added beyond the due; goods taken from the enemy.

mots.racine: أ ل ه / ر س ل
mots.def: first ownership belongs to God and the Prophet.

mots.racine: ص ل ح / ب ي ن
mots.sens: set right what is between you
mots.def: to mend what stands between people, restore harmony.
those who have entered into faith.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ اللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَإِذَا تُلِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُهُ زَادَتْهُمْ إِيمَانًا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ
versets.tr: innamā l-muʾminūna lladhīna idhā dhukira llāhu wajilat qulūbuhum wa-idhā tuliyat ʿalayhim āyātuhu zādathum īmānan wa-ʿalā rabbihim yatawakkalūn
versets.trad: The true believers are only those whose hearts tremble when God is mentioned, whose faith increases when His verses are recited to them, and who place their trust in their Lord,
versets.commentaire: Wajl is the intensity of fear that wrenches one from heedlessness and dislodges the soul from the dwellings of forgetfulness. Qushayrī: when they detach themselves from the valleys of dispersion and return to the gatherings of remembrance, they find in God a stillness — and what is recited to them then adds attestation upon attestation, realization upon realization. The master sees here the divine custom toward the knowers: to pass them between the radiance of majesty that makes hearts tremble and the gentleness of beauty that calms them; thus the remembrance of separation extinguishes them and the remembrance of union revives them.

mots.def: wajl: fear that seizes and penetrates.

mots.racine: ز ي د / أ م ن
mots.def: faith grows through hearing the Word.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yuqīmūna ṣ-ṣalāta wa-mimmā razaqnāhum yunfiqūn
versets.trad: those who establish the prayer and spend, in alms, from what We have provided them.
versets.commentaire: Three traits seal faith: the tremor, the increase, and the fulfilment. Qushayrī: they accept no negligence in their works, resign themselves to no wealth gained outside the lawful, and never tarry in the dwellings of falling short. The Law finds nothing to reproach them, and the Reality affords them no excuse to halt.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
mots.def: iqāma: to hold upright, to perform fully.

mots.sens: from what We have provided them
mots.def: rizq: every provision coming from God.
infāq: to distribute, give without holding back.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ۚ لَّهُمْ دَرَجَاتٌ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ
versets.tr: ulāʾika humu l-muʾminūna ḥaqqan, lahum darajātun ʿinda rabbihim wa-maghfiratun wa-rizqun karīm
versets.trad: Those, truly, are the believers. For them are ranks with their Lord, and forgiveness, and a generous provision.
versets.commentaire: "Truly believers": Qushayrī says they have verified the true by the true and attested the sincere by the sincere. The ranks are measured for them according to what eternal solicitude inscribed for them, then according to the sincerity of their service. And the forgiveness — God veils the flaws of sinners so they do not despair of His bounty, and veils too the virtues of the knowers so they do not become enamoured of their own states. As for the noble provision, it is what comes without being reckoned, and does not distract the servant from the contemplation of the Provider.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ك ر م
mots.def: a generous gift free of stain.

versets.ar: كَمَا أَخْرَجَكَ رَبُّكَ مِن بَيْتِكَ بِالْحَقِّ وَإِنَّ فَرِيقًا مِّنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَكَارِهُونَ
versets.tr: kamā akhrajaka rabbuka min baytika bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-inna farīqan mina l-muʾminīna la-kārihūn
versets.trad: Just as your Lord brought you forth from your home for the Truth, though a party of the believers were averse,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls a law of the prophets and saints: God opens to them the fullness of His grace only after they have torn themselves from familiar dwellings and stripped themselves of every share in the known. The Prophet's going forth to Badr — against the inclination of several — is the outward sign of an inner law: none attains the noble gifts save by draining the cup of trials.

mots.def: ikhrāj: to bring out, to send forth.
according to truth, the just decree.

versets.ar: يُجَادِلُونَكَ فِي الْحَقِّ بَعْدَمَا تَبَيَّنَ كَأَنَّمَا يُسَاقُونَ إِلَى الْمَوْتِ وَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ
versets.tr: yujādilūnaka fī l-ḥaqqi baʿdamā tabayyana ka-annamā yusāqūna ilā l-mawti wa-hum yanẓurūn
versets.trad: disputing with you about the Truth after it had been made clear, as though they were being driven to death while they looked on.
versets.commentaire: To dispute the Truth after its proof has shone is, according to Qushayrī, the mark of an arrogance that freezes its bearer, condemns him to the dread of his own straying, and makes life so bitter that he longs for its end. Such is the state of one who sees the evidence yet refuses it: driven toward what he fears, eyes open upon his own destruction.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ يَعِدُكُمُ اللَّهُ إِحْدَى الطَّائِفَتَيْنِ أَنَّهَا لَكُمْ وَتَوَدُّونَ أَنَّ غَيْرَ ذَاتِ الشَّوْكَةِ تَكُونُ لَكُمْ وَيُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَن يُحِقَّ الْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَاتِهِ وَيَقْطَعَ دَابِرَ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh yaʿidukumu llāhu iḥdā ṭ-ṭāʾifatayni annahā lakum wa-tawaddūna anna ghayra dhāti sh-shawkati takūnu lakum wa-yurīdu llāhu an yuḥiqqa l-ḥaqqa bi-kalimātihi wa-yaqṭaʿa dābira l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: And when God promised you that one of the two parties would be yours, you wished that the unarmed one might be yours. But God willed to confirm the Truth by His words and to cut off the last remnant of the disbelievers,
versets.commentaire: The soul by nature prefers the gentler share; but the fullness of gifts is won only at the price of trials. Qushayrī: when God wills to consecrate a servant to His nearness, He decrees the decline of his soul's assaults, the withering of some of his passions, and causes the suns of the Realities to rise upon him. Thus the unarmed caravan — preferred by men — was not their lot; it was battle, and the victory of the Word over illusion.

mots.sens: the last remnant of the disbelievers
mots.def: dābir: the hindmost remnant, the root.

versets.ar: لِيُحِقَّ الْحَقَّ وَيُبْطِلَ الْبَاطِلَ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُجْرِمُونَ
versets.tr: li-yuḥiqqa l-ḥaqqa wa-yubṭila l-bāṭila wa-law kariha l-mujrimūn
versets.trad: that He might confirm the Truth and bring the false to nothing, however much the guilty should hate it.
versets.commentaire: To confirm the Truth is to unfurl the banners of union; to bring the false to nothing is to subdue the portions of frivolity. The Truth is established not only in the outward event, but in the heart where it sweeps away all that is not itself.

versets.ar: إِذْ تَسْتَغِيثُونَ رَبَّكُمْ فَاسْتَجَابَ لَكُمْ أَنِّي مُمِدُّكُم بِأَلْفٍ مِّنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ مُرْدِفِينَ
versets.tr: idh tastaghīthūna rabbakum fa-stajāba lakum annī mumiddukum bi-alfin mina l-malāʾikati murdifīn
versets.trad: When you cried to your Lord for help, He answered you: "I am reinforcing you with a thousand angels, following one upon another."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: imploring grows in measure of the sight of one's own destitution and the absence of any power of one's own — and the certainty that the Real alone dispenses is itself the realization of what is asked. When the call is sincere, the answer hastens. Such is the noble custom: the seeker finds, but by the Giver's gift; the yearner arrives, but to His bounties; the path is easy, but toward the finding of His gentleness.

versets.ar: وَمَا جَعَلَهُ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بُشْرَىٰ وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ بِهِ قُلُوبُكُمْ ۚ وَمَا النَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā jaʿalahu llāhu illā bushrā wa-li-taṭmaʾinna bihi qulūbukum, wa-mā n-naṣru illā min ʿindi llāh, inna llāha ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: God made this only as good tidings, and that your hearts might be at peace by it. Victory comes only from God. Truly God is Mighty, Wise.
versets.commentaire: The angelic reinforcement was first an announcement, then a stilling; but God did not let the believers rest in the means — He raised their gaze from the gift to the Giver: victory is only from God. Qushayrī: deliverance from the trial is granted, the ways of aid are unbroken, the calls are heard and the answer is never withheld — yet God remains the Inaccessible beyond every joining and every parting.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.def: two divine Names: Omnipotence and Wisdom.

versets.ar: إِذْ يُغَشِّيكُمُ النُّعَاسَ أَمَنَةً مِّنْهُ وَيُنَزِّلُ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً لِّيُطَهِّرَكُم بِهِ وَيُذْهِبَ عَنكُمْ رِجْزَ الشَّيْطَانِ وَلِيَرْبِطَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِكُمْ وَيُثَبِّتَ بِهِ الْأَقْدَامَ
versets.tr: idh yughashshīkumu n-nuʿāsa amanatan minhu wa-yunazzilu ʿalaykum mina s-samāʾi māʾan li-yuṭahhirakum bihi wa-yudhhiba ʿankum rijza sh-shayṭāni wa-li-yarbiṭa ʿalā qulūbikum wa-yuthabbita bihi l-aqdām
versets.trad: Remember when He covered you with slumber as a security from Him, and sent down upon you water from the sky to purify you by it, to remove from you the defilement of Satan, to fortify your hearts and to make firm your steps.
versets.commentaire: The sleep of Badr was a grace: it removed from outward forms and souls the fatigue of strangers, and sent down upon the hearts a spirit of security. The rain purified the bodies, hardened the sand beneath their feet, and drove off the breath of Satan. Qushayrī adds the inner reading: just as He purified their outward forms with the water of the sky, He purified their secrets with the water of realization — withdrawing them from the sight of every other and every secondary cause — and bound their hearts by the contemplation of the unfolding decree.

mots.racine: غ ش و / ن ع س

mots.def: a state of being safe from fear.

mots.racine: ر ج ز / ش ط ن

mots.sens: that He bind firmness upon your hearts
mots.def: rabṭ ʿalā: to steady the heart.

mots.racine: ث ب ت / ق د م
mots.sens: that He make firm the steps

versets.ar: إِذْ يُوحِي رَبُّكَ إِلَى الْمَلَائِكَةِ أَنِّي مَعَكُمْ فَثَبِّتُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۚ سَأُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ فَاضْرِبُوا فَوْقَ الْأَعْنَاقِ وَاضْرِبُوا مِنْهُمْ كُلَّ بَنَانٍ
versets.tr: idh yūḥī rabbuka ilā l-malāʾikati annī maʿakum fa-thabbitū lladhīna āmanū, sa-ulqī fī qulūbi lladhīna kafarū r-ruʿba fa-ḍribū fawqa l-aʿnāqi wa-ḍribū minhum kulla banān
versets.trad: When your Lord revealed to the angels: "I am with you, so make firm those who believe. I shall cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve; strike, then, above the necks, and strike off from them every fingertip!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: just as God conveys the whisperings of Satan to the heart, He conveys the inspiration of the angel — and He aided the believers by casting fright and terror into the hearts of the disbelievers. The angels made the men firm sometimes by appearing in human form, sometimes by depositing certainty in the hearts without being recognized.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ شَاقُّوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ۚ وَمَن يُشَاقِقِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahum shāqqū llāha wa-rasūlahu, wa-man yushāqiqi llāha wa-rasūlahu fa-inna llāha shadīdu l-ʿiqāb
versets.trad: That was because they had broken with God and His Messenger. Whoever breaks with God and His Messenger — truly God is severe in punishment.
versets.commentaire: To break with, here, is to take a side against the side of God: to separate by opposing. Qushayrī: He grants the guilty a respite of some days, then leaves him no more; He makes him taste the rigour of his deed and dispels the illusion of his conjectures.

mots.def: shiqāq: to split off by setting oneself against.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ق ب
mots.def: ʿiqāb: penalty that follows the deed.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكُمْ فَذُوقُوهُ وَأَنَّ لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابَ النَّارِ
versets.tr: dhālikum fa-dhūqūhu wa-anna li-l-kāfirīna ʿadhāba n-nār
versets.trad: That is yours, so taste it! And know that for the disbelievers is the punishment of the Fire.
versets.commentaire: Two punishments for the rebellious: one in ready coin — paid here below — the other in promise for the hereafter. Taste then, says Qushayrī, what is hastened, and know that what is deferred is more terrible still.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ن و ر
mots.def: the suffering of the infernal blaze.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا لَقِيتُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا زَحْفًا فَلَا تُوَلُّوهُمُ الْأَدْبَارَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā laqītumu lladhīna kafarū zaḥfan fa-lā tuwallūhumu l-adbār
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you meet the disbelievers advancing for battle, do not turn your backs to them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens here the inner reading of combat: outwardly, it is firmness before the assault; inwardly, it is the struggle against Satan, and the duty is to halt before his calls to stumbling; and it is also the struggle against the soul — when the servant halts before the soul's call to its caprices and does not obey its desire, he has rendered the struggle its due. Flight, at all these levels, is the same: to turn from the Face of the Real toward the shadow of oneself.

mots.racine: و ل ي / د ب ر
mots.sens: you turn your backs to them

versets.ar: وَمَن يُوَلِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ دُبُرَهُ إِلَّا مُتَحَرِّفًا لِّقِتَالٍ أَوْ مُتَحَيِّزًا إِلَىٰ فِئَةٍ فَقَدْ بَاءَ بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَمَأْوَاهُ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-man yuwallihim yawmaʾidhin duburahu illā mutaḥarrifan li-qitālin aw mutaḥayyizan ilā fiʾatin fa-qad bāʾa bi-ghaḍabin mina llāhi wa-maʾwāhu jahannamu wa-biʾsa l-maṣīr
versets.trad: Whoever turns his back to them that day — unless wheeling for battle or rallying to a troop — has incurred wrath from God, and his refuge is Hell. What a wretched journey's end!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the allusion in "wheeling for battle" is the lightening through certain dispensations so as to better sustain the harder — like eating what keeps one upright in order to have strength for the vigil. And "rallying to a troop" denotes the support the disciple takes in the company of his peers and the masters: the disciple is nourished by the aspiration of his shaykh, as the poor by the wealth of the rich.

mots.racine: و ل ي / د ب ر
ح ر ف / ق ت ل
ح و ز / ف أ و
ب و أ / غ ض ب

mots.def: the place where one comes to rest.

versets.ar: فَلَمْ تَقْتُلُوهُمْ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ قَتَلَهُمْ ۚ وَمَا رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ رَمَىٰ ۚ وَلِيُبْلِيَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْهُ بَلَاءً حَسَنًا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-lam taqtulūhum wa-lākinna llāha qatalahum, wa-mā ramayta idh ramayta wa-lākinna llāha ramā, wa-li-yubliya l-muʾminīna minhu balāʾan ḥasanan, inna llāha samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: It was not you who slew them, but God slew them. And you did not throw when you threw, but God threw — that He might test the believers with a fair trial from Him. Truly God is Hearing, Knowing.
versets.commentaire: The verse of jamʿ par excellence. Qushayrī: "when you threw" is separation — the attribute of servanthood; "but God threw" is union — the attribute of Lordship. Every separation not paired with union, and every union not confirmed by separation in the servant, leaves its bearer off balance. The "fair trial" is the testing to which God Himself gives the means to respond: the grace of gratitude in the gift, and the realization of patience in the affliction — and every trial in which one beholds the Tester within the trial itself is fair.

mots.def: ibtilāʾ: to put to the test.

mots.racine: ب ل و / ح س ن
mots.def: a trial from which good emerges.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكُمْ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ مُوهِنُ كَيْدِ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: dhālikum wa-anna llāha mūhinu kaydi l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: That is so; and that God renders feeble the scheming of the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: God weakens the scheming by fortifying the hearts of believers with the light of certainty and steadiness in awaiting grace, and by seizing the disbelievers from where they do not expect.

mots.def: wahn: weakness; ihān: to render feeble.

versets.ar: إِن تَسْتَفْتِحُوا فَقَدْ جَاءَكُمُ الْفَتْحُ ۖ وَإِن تَنتَهُوا فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن تَعُودُوا نَعُدْ وَلَن تُغْنِيَ عَنكُمْ فِئَتُكُمْ شَيْئًا وَلَوْ كَثُرَتْ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: in tastaftiḥū fa-qad jāʾakumu l-fatḥ, wa-in tantahū fa-huwa khayrun lakum, wa-in taʿūdū naʿud wa-lan tughniya ʿankum fiʾatukum shayʾan wa-law kathurat wa-anna llāha maʿa l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: If you sought a verdict, the verdict has now come to you. If you desist, it is better for you; but if you return, We too shall return, and your host will avail you nothing, however numerous, for God is with the believers.
versets.commentaire: The idolaters had prayed: "Lord, give victory to the dearer of the two camps to You"; He answered their prayer and gave victory to the one He loved — not them. Qushayrī: by their own choice they asked their own ruin; they imagined they merited nearness while they were at the heart of separation. When the veil fell, they knew their conjecture had led them astray. And the law stands: whoever is seized by the power of the One, the multitude of number avails him nothing.

mots.def: istiftāḥ: to call for the opening, to seek to prevail.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَلَا تَوَلَّوْا عَنْهُ وَأَنتُمْ تَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū aṭīʿū llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-lā tawallaw ʿanhu wa-antum tasmaʿūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Obey God and His Messenger, and do not turn away from him while you hear.
themes: do not turn away after hearing
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes a grammatical subtlety: He said "obey God and His Messenger" without repeating the verb — a discreet distinction, an elevation that withdraws from expression and remains barely indicated. The obedient are of several ranks: one through fear of punishment, another through hope of reward, another through realization of servanthood, another through the honour of Lordship — and what a distance between obedient and obedient!

versets.ar: وَلَا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ قَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā takūnū ka-lladhīna qālū samiʿnā wa-hum lā yasmaʿūn
versets.trad: And do not be like those who say, "We have heard," while they do not listen.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: do not be of those who bear witness openly and deny in secret; do not confess with the tongue while remaining in your ingratitude. Whoever speaks by deceit, experience testifies against him of his lie.

versets.ar: إِنَّ شَرَّ الدَّوَابِّ عِندَ اللَّهِ الصُّمُّ الْبُكْمُ الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: inna sharra d-dawābbi ʿinda llāhi ṣ-ṣummu l-bukmu lladhīna lā yaʿqilūn
versets.trad: The worst of living creatures in the sight of God are the deaf and dumb who do not reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the callings of the Real speak through the beauty of evidence, the tongues of proof are truthful, the intuitions of the Unseen scatter the darkness of doubt — whoever becomes deaf to what speaks to his secret, blind to what is unveiled to his heart, dumb to that to which his understanding is guided: his measure is below the beasts, and his abasement above all measure in the judgment of God.

mots.def: dābba: every being that moves on earth.

mots.racine: ص م م / ب ك م

mots.def: ʿaql: the intelligence that binds and discerns.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ عَلِمَ اللَّهُ فِيهِمْ خَيْرًا لَّأَسْمَعَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ أَسْمَعَهُمْ لَتَوَلَّوا وَّهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law ʿalima llāhu fīhim khayran la-asmaʿahum, wa-law asmaʿahum la-tawallaw wa-hum muʿriḍūn
versets.trad: Had God known any good in them, He would have made them hear; but had He made them hear, they would have turned away in aversion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whomever the antecedents of the apportioning have set aside, the consequences of service do not bring near; whomever God knows beforehand by the trait of wretchedness, He withholds from him what would make His pardon due. Had they been within the reach of mercy, He would have clothed them in the mantle of preservation.

mots.sens: He would have made them hear

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اسْتَجِيبُوا لِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ لِمَا يُحْيِيكُمْ ۖ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَحُولُ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَقَلْبِهِ وَأَنَّهُ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū stajībū li-llāhi wa-li-r-rasūli idhā daʿākum li-mā yuḥyīkum, wa-ʿlamū anna llāha yaḥūlu bayna l-marʾi wa-qalbihi wa-annahu ilayhi tuḥsharūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Respond to God and to the Messenger when He calls you to that which gives you life. And know that God comes between a man and his heart, and that to Him you shall be gathered.
themes: responding to the call that gives life
God between a man and his heart
versets.commentaire: A central verse, savoured with fervour by the Sufis. Qushayrī distinguishes the modes of "giving life": the worshippers, He gives them life through His obedience after extinguishing them to His disobedience; the savants, through the proofs of His Lordship after extinguishing them to ignorance; the believers, through the light of His connivance after extinguishing them by the swords of their inner struggle; the Unifiers, through the light of His tawḥīd after extinguishing them to all perception of any other than Him. And the overwhelming word: He comes between a man and his heart so they have no recourse but to Him — when an event befalls, they have no way toward the contingent, no reliance on their own heart.

mots.def: istijāba: total response, free of constraint.

mots.racine: م ر أ / ق ل ب
mots.sens: between a man and his heart
mots.def: the very intimacy of one's being.

versets.ar: وَاتَّقُوا فِتْنَةً لَّا تُصِيبَنَّ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنكُمْ خَاصَّةً ۖ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: wa-ttaqū fitnatan lā tuṣībanna lladhīna ẓalamū minkum khāṣṣatan, wa-ʿlamū anna llāha shadīdu l-ʿiqāb
versets.trad: And fear a trial that will not strike only those of you who have done wrong. And know that God is severe in punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the sense: when the elders keep silent before the failings of the younger, the trial strikes them too; and when the ascetic descends to the dispensations of the Law to seize a surplus of the world — even by lawful means — his fault spreads to those who follow him. The verse teaches that the knowers themselves, if they return to what flatters them, the disciple looks upon them and slackens in turn in the sincerity of his struggle.

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ أَنتُمْ قَلِيلٌ مُّسْتَضْعَفُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ تَخَافُونَ أَن يَتَخَطَّفَكُمُ النَّاسُ فَآوَاكُمْ وَأَيَّدَكُم بِنَصْرِهِ وَرَزَقَكُم مِّنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-dhkurū idh antum qalīlun mustaḍʿafūna fī l-arḍi takhāfūna an yatakhaṭṭafakumu n-nāsu fa-āwākum wa-ayyadakum bi-naṣrihi wa-razaqakum mina ṭ-ṭayyibāti laʿallakum tashkurūn
versets.trad: And remember when you were few, deemed weak in the land, fearing that men would snatch you away; then He sheltered you, strengthened you with His aid, and provided you with good things, that you might be grateful.
versets.commentaire: The reminder serves as antidote to forgetfulness. Qushayrī: He nourishes the bodies and the outward forms with good provisions, and nourishes the spirits and the secrets with modes of light. The truth of gratitude for these gifts is absence to them through engulfment in the contemplation of the Bestower.

mots.def: istiḍʿāf: to be treated as weak.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَخُونُوا اللَّهَ وَالرَّسُولَ وَتَخُونُوا أَمَانَاتِكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā takhūnū llāha wa-r-rasūla wa-takhūnū amānātikum wa-antum taʿlamūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not betray God and the Messenger, nor knowingly betray the trusts confided to you.
versets.commentaire: Betrayal, says Qushayrī, is the inward harbouring of what contradicts what is legitimately expected of you. To betray God is to squander what He has made you keeper of — by going against sincerity in His religion; to betray the Prophet is to depart in secret from what one affects to embrace in public. The most subtle betrayal: in works, the claim that they come from you without referring them to God; in states, your gaze upon them instead of absence to them in the contemplation of the Real.

versets.ar: وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَأَوْلَادُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ عِندَهُ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-ʿlamū annamā amwālukum wa-awlādukum fitnatun wa-anna llāha ʿindahu ajrun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: And know that your wealth and your children are a trial, and that with God is a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: wealth is a trial when it distracts you from God; children are a trial when, for their sake, you neglect the right of God. Wealth, in the measure of sufficiency and continence, is a grace; in the aim of excess and vainglory, a fitna. In sum, all that turns you from God is fitna.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ع ظ م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِن تَتَّقُوا اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّكُمْ فُرْقَانًا وَيُكَفِّرْ عَنكُمْ سَيِّئَاتِكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū in tattaqū llāha yajʿal lakum furqānan wa-yukaffir ʿankum sayyiʾātikum wa-yaghfir lakum, wa-llāhu dhū l-faḍli l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: O you who believe! If you fear God, He will grant you a discernment, efface your evil deeds from you, and forgive you. God is the Master of immense bounty.
versets.commentaire: Furqān: that by which the true and the false are distinguished. Qushayrī sharply separates two orders: the savants have their furqān by sought-out proof; the knowers have their furqān by gifted knowledge. The first live by the fruit of their efforts, the second by the bounty of their Lord. And the closing formula: knowledge is a designation come from God; effacement, a lightening come from God; forgiveness, an honour bestowed on the servant by God.

mots.def: furqān: that which separates true from false.
takfīr: to cover over, to efface.

mots.racine: ف ض ل / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِيُثْبِتُوكَ أَوْ يَقْتُلُوكَ أَوْ يُخْرِجُوكَ ۚ وَيَمْكُرُونَ وَيَمْكُرُ اللَّهُ ۖ وَاللَّهُ خَيْرُ الْمَاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh yamkuru bika lladhīna kafarū li-yuthbitūka aw yaqtulūka aw yukhrijūka, wa-yamkurūna wa-yamkuru llāhu, wa-llāhu khayru l-mākirīn
versets.trad: And remember when the disbelievers schemed against you to confine you, or kill you, or drive you out — they schemed, and God schemed, and God is the best of schemers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: makr is to display benevolence while secretly intending harm; God's makr is the requital of makr. His ruse against them is to cast into their hearts that He is doing them good, while in reality He punishes them — He occupies them with the world until they forget the other Abode, and that is His ruse, for they settle into it and He seizes them suddenly from where they do not sense it. Such too is the most subtle ruse: the illusion given to a people by a fair renown and the flow of pious works upon them, while their secrets are bound to the others-than-God, and they imagine they are near.

mots.def: ithbāt here: to bind, to detain.

mots.racine: خ ي ر / م ك ر
mots.def: the one whose makr surpasses all other.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا قَالُوا قَدْ سَمِعْنَا لَوْ نَشَاءُ لَقُلْنَا مِثْلَ هَٰذَا ۙ إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا أَسَاطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātunā qālū qad samiʿnā law nashāʾu la-qulnā mithla hādhā, in hādhā illā asāṭīru l-awwalīn
versets.trad: And when Our verses are recited to them, they say, "We have heard. Had we wished, we could say the like of this. This is nothing but the legends of the ancients."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the extremity of their ignorance and the ill of their denial veiled from their minds the ugliness of their claim to be able to imitate the Qur'an; and the test exposed them: lacking proof, powerless of tongue, despite their boasts of eloquence. And the universal lesson: whoever looked upon the Qur'an with a belittling eye was deprived of the blessings of understanding.

mots.racine: س ط ر / أ و ل

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالُوا اللَّهُمَّ إِن كَانَ هَٰذَا هُوَ الْحَقَّ مِنْ عِندِكَ فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنَا حِجَارَةً مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ أَوِ ائْتِنَا بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-idh qālū llāhumma in kāna hādhā huwa l-ḥaqqa min ʿindika fa-amṭir ʿalaynā ḥijāratan mina s-samāʾi awi ʾtinā bi-ʿadhābin alīm
versets.trad: And when they said, "O God, if this is the Truth from You, then rain down upon us stones from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment."
versets.commentaire: This demand, Qushayrī observes, is the clearest proof that they had hardened their hearts upon denying the Messenger: they were certain, deep down, that their prayer against themselves would not be answered. And the lesson: the soul's quietude in a thing is not knowledge — for it is found with knowledge as it is found with ignorance.

mots.def: imṭār: to make fall like rain.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنتَ فِيهِمْ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ مُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna llāhu li-yuʿadhdhibahum wa-anta fīhim, wa-mā kāna llāhu muʿadhdhibahum wa-hum yastaghfirūn
versets.trad: But God would not punish them while you are among them, nor would God punish them while they seek forgiveness.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God was not to punish them while you are among them, nor their forebears while you were in their loins, nor them today while you are in their midst — out of reverence for your rank. And were you to leave them, He would not punish them so long as there remained among them Your servants who seek forgiveness. And the noble principle: if the presence of the Messenger among the disbelievers prevents punishment, how much more does the presence of gnosis in the hearts have a right to avert punishment.

versets.ar: وَمَا لَهُمْ أَلَّا يُعَذِّبَهُمُ اللَّهُ وَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ عَنِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ وَمَا كَانُوا أَوْلِيَاءَهُ ۚ إِنْ أَوْلِيَاؤُهُ إِلَّا الْمُتَّقُونَ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā lahum allā yuʿadhdhibahumu llāhu wa-hum yaṣuddūna ʿani l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi wa-mā kānū awliyāʾahu, in awliyāʾuhu illā l-muttaqūna wa-lākinna aktharahum lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: And what have they that God should not punish them, while they bar from the Sacred Mosque and are not its guardians? Its guardians are only the God-fearing, but most of them do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: His friends (awliyāʾ) are only the God-fearing — and those are the ones who have guarded themselves from association. The verse distinguishes apparent possession of the Sanctuary from its true guardianship: the house belongs to the one who serves it, not to the one who bars it. The believer, even tried by his fault, does not abide forever in punishment; but the disbeliever, set apart from divine friendship, has no share in pardon.

mots.racine: س ج د / ح ر م

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ صَلَاتُهُمْ عِندَ الْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مُكَاءً وَتَصْدِيَةً ۚ فَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna ṣalātuhum ʿinda l-bayti illā mukāʾan wa-taṣdiya, fa-dhūqū l-ʿadhāba bimā kuntum takfurūn
versets.trad: Their prayer at the House was nothing but whistling and clapping of hands. So taste the punishment for your disbelief!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī cuts to the heart: their outward works stripped themselves of the sincerity of their beliefs; God found in them nothing to count. For the purity of speech is only with the limpidity of the state, and the toil of the visible is accepted only with the light of the secrets. The punishment is twofold: hastened — their illusion of being upon something — and deferred for the hereafter.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُمْ لِيَصُدُّوا عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ فَسَيُنفِقُونَهَا ثُمَّ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسْرَةً ثُمَّ يُغْلَبُونَ ۗ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ يُحْشَرُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū yunfiqūna amwālahum li-yaṣuddū ʿan sabīli llāhi, fa-sa-yunfiqūnahā thumma takūnu ʿalayhim ḥasratan thumma yughlabūn, wa-lladhīna kafarū ilā jahannama yuḥsharūn
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve spend their wealth to bar from the way of God. They will spend it, then it will be a source of grief to them, then they will be overcome. And those who disbelieve will be gathered to Hell,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: by their expenditures they aim at the consolidation and ordering of their affairs, but they gain only loss and arrive only at lack. They have lost without knowing it; they have failed, and soon will know. And their wealth, even were they to surrender it, does not save them: their destiny remains humiliation.

versets.ar: لِيَمِيزَ اللَّهُ الْخَبِيثَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبِ وَيَجْعَلَ الْخَبِيثَ بَعْضَهُ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ فَيَرْكُمَهُ جَمِيعًا فَيَجْعَلَهُ فِي جَهَنَّمَ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: li-yamīza llāhu l-khabītha mina ṭ-ṭayyibi wa-yajʿala l-khabītha baʿḍahu ʿalā baʿḍin fa-yarkumahu jamīʿan fa-yajʿalahu fī jahannama, ulāʾika humu l-khāsirūn
versets.trad: that God may separate the foul from the good, and pile the foul one upon another, heaping them all together and casting them into Hell. Those are the losers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the pairs: the foul is what is not fit for God; the good is what is fit for God. The foul is what the Law has judged ugly; the good is what knowledge has witnessed beautiful. The foul is what occupies its owner away from God; the good is what leads to God. The foul is what one takes for one's own share; the good is what one spends by the command of God.

versets.ar: قُل لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِن يَنتَهُوا يُغْفَرْ لَهُم مَّا قَدْ سَلَفَ وَإِن يَعُودُوا فَقَدْ مَضَتْ سُنَّتُ الْأَوَّلِينَ
versets.tr: qul li-lladhīna kafarū in yantahū yughfar lahum mā qad salafa wa-in yaʿūdū fa-qad maḍat sunnatu l-awwalīn
versets.trad: Say to those who disbelieve: if they desist, what is past will be forgiven them; but if they return, then the way of the ancients has already gone before.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī winds out a cascade of promises: if they hold back the bridle of rebellion, We remove from them the smallness of humiliation and grant them the spirit of security; if they loosen the belt of defiance, We release from them the fetters of distance; if they see the ugliness of their deeds, We lavish upon them the mending of their states; if they incline toward excuse, We cast upon them the beauty of indulgence; if they return to repentance, We open to them the grace of increase.

mots.racine: س ن ن / أ و ل

versets.ar: وَقَاتِلُوهُمْ حَتَّىٰ لَا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ وَيَكُونَ الدِّينُ كُلُّهُ لِلَّهِ ۚ فَإِنِ انتَهَوْا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-qātilūhum ḥattā lā takūna fitnatun wa-yakūna d-dīnu kulluhu li-llāhi, fa-ini ntahaw fa-inna llāha bimā yaʿmalūna baṣīr
versets.trad: And fight them until there is no more sedition and the religion is wholly for God. But if they desist, then God sees well what they do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: fight them with rigour until their root is uprooted and the believers are safe from their harm — like the serpent of the valley, of which none can think himself safe so long as a motion remains in it. Inner reading: that in the soil of the heart no shoot of hidden idolatry should sprout any longer.

mots.def: divine Name: the One who sees all.

versets.ar: وَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ مَوْلَاكُمْ ۚ نِعْمَ الْمَوْلَىٰ وَنِعْمَ النَّصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-in tawallaw fa-ʿlamū anna llāha mawlākum, niʿma l-mawlā wa-niʿma n-naṣīr
versets.trad: And if they turn away, then know that God is your Protector. What an excellent Protector, and what an excellent Helper!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī weaves the luminous pairs: what an excellent Protector for you on the day of the apportioning of gnosis, and what an excellent Helper on the day of the grace of pardon! What an excellent Protector when you were not, and what an excellent Helper when you came to be! What an excellent Protector through designation before obligation, and what an excellent Helper through lightening and multiplying — He lightens your sins and multiplies your good deeds.

mots.def: Name: the One who helps with strength.

versets.ar: وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا غَنِمْتُم مِّن شَيْءٍ فَأَنَّ لِلَّهِ خُمُسَهُ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِي الْقُرْبَىٰ وَالْيَتَامَىٰ وَالْمَسَاكِينِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ إِن كُنتُمْ آمَنتُم بِاللَّهِ وَمَا أَنزَلْنَا عَلَىٰ عَبْدِنَا يَوْمَ الْفُرْقَانِ يَوْمَ الْتَقَى الْجَمْعَانِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-ʿlamū annamā ghanimtum min shayʾin fa-anna li-llāhi khumusahu wa-li-r-rasūli wa-li-dhī l-qurbā wa-l-yatāmā wa-l-masākīni wa-bni s-sabīli in kuntum āmantum bi-llāhi wa-mā anzalnā ʿalā ʿabdinā yawma l-furqāni yawma ltaqā l-jamʿān, wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: And know that whatever spoils you take, a fifth of it belongs to God and the Messenger, to the near kin, the orphans, the needy and the wayfarer, if you believe in God and in what We sent down upon Our servant on the Day of Discernment, the day the two hosts met. And God is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens here his great commentary on the two jihāds: there are two struggles: the outer jihād against the disbelievers, and the inner jihād against the soul and the devil — which is the greater jihād, as the hadith relates. Just as in the lesser jihād the spoils fall to the victor, in the greater jihād there is a spoil: that the servant retake possession of his soul, which was in the hands of the enemy — passion and the devil. And the fifth for God, in this tongue of allusion, is a portion exclusive to God in which the servant has no share: neither of the noble gifts of the Other World, nor of the fruits of nearness, nor of the graces of welcome — then the servant becomes freed from all bondage to a share, pure for God by God, effacing all other than God.

mots.def: khums: the share of God and the rightful claimants.
the day of Badr, when true and false were parted.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ج م ع
mots.def: the meeting of the two armies.

versets.ar: إِذْ أَنتُم بِالْعُدْوَةِ الدُّنْيَا وَهُم بِالْعُدْوَةِ الْقُصْوَىٰ وَالرَّكْبُ أَسْفَلَ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَلَوْ تَوَاعَدتُّمْ لَاخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِي الْمِيعَادِ ۙ وَلَٰكِن لِّيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا لِّيَهْلِكَ مَنْ هَلَكَ عَن بَيِّنَةٍ وَيَحْيَىٰ مَنْ حَيَّ عَن بَيِّنَةٍ ۗ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَسَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: idh antum bi-l-ʿudwati d-dunyā wa-hum bi-l-ʿudwati l-quṣwā wa-r-rakbu asfala minkum, wa-law tawāʿadtum la-khtalaftum fī l-mīʿādi wa-lākin li-yaqḍiya llāhu amran kāna mafʿūlan li-yahlika man halaka ʿan bayyinatin wa-yaḥyā man ḥayya ʿan bayyinatin, wa-inna llāha la-samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: When you were on the nearer slope and they on the farther slope, and the caravan below you. Had you made an appointment, you would have differed about the time. But it was so that God might decree a matter already accomplished, that whoever perished might perish upon clear evidence, and whoever lived might live upon clear evidence. And truly God is Hearing, Knowing.
themes: to perish and to live upon evidence
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God informs that what passed at Badr was not by your arrangement, nor by your deliberation; rather, had the matter been left to you, you would have been compelled and would have fled it. It was as it was, so that God might fulfil a decree already made. And the inner reading: he perishes who strays from the Real after holding to it a while, and he is guided who stands upon the Real after the clearing of the proof. The perishing one is he who remained bound to his share; the living one, he who was ravished from all share.

mots.racine: ع د و / د ن و

versets.ar: إِذْ يُرِيكَهُمُ اللَّهُ فِي مَنَامِكَ قَلِيلًا ۖ وَلَوْ أَرَاكَهُمْ كَثِيرًا لَّفَشِلْتُمْ وَلَتَنَازَعْتُمْ فِي الْأَمْرِ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ سَلَّمَ ۗ إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: idh yurīkahumu llāhu fī manāmika qalīlan, wa-law arākahum kathīran la-fashiltum wa-la-tanāzaʿtum fī l-amri wa-lākinna llāha sallama, innahu ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūr
versets.trad: Remember when God showed them to you in your dream as few; had He shown them to you as many, you would have lost heart and quarrelled about the matter. But God kept you safe. Truly He knows what is within the breasts.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God willed that this battle should take place between them at Badr; and when God wills a thing, He arranges its causes — He made the disbelievers few in the eyes of the Muslims so they would take courage, and made the Muslims few in the eyes of the disbelievers so they would advance to battle with assurance, and with more smallness in the decree of God.

mots.racine: ذ و ت / ص د ر

versets.ar: وَإِذْ يُرِيكُمُوهُمْ إِذِ الْتَقَيْتُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِكُمْ قَلِيلًا وَيُقَلِّلُكُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِهِمْ لِيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا ۗ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ
versets.tr: wa-idh yurīkumūhum idhi ltaqaytum fī aʿyunikum qalīlan wa-yuqallilukum fī aʿyunihim li-yaqḍiya llāhu amran kāna mafʿūlan, wa-ilā llāhi turjaʿu l-umūr
versets.trad: And when you met, He made them few in your eyes and made you few in their eyes, so that God might decree a matter already accomplished. And to God all matters return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when God wills to aid a servant, even if all mankind plotted against him and aimed at him with all their harm, no one who desires his ruin profits by a single effort; and a wall rises between him and the attaining of His gentleness. And when He wills evil for a servant, there is no turning back, effort avails nothing, and no effort raises him once he has fallen in the decree of God.

mots.racine: ر ج ع / أ م ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا لَقِيتُمْ فِئَةً فَاثْبُتُوا وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā laqītum fiʾatan fa-thbutū wa-dhkurū llāha kathīran laʿallakum tufliḥūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you meet a troop, stand firm and remember God much, that you may prosper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: firmness is found only with the strength of the heart and the intensity of certainty; and that comes only with the penetration of insight, the realization by God, and the seeing of all occurrences as coming from Him. There, one surrenders to God and accepts His decree — and for that He referred them to remembrance. All goods, he says, are in the firmness of the heart; and by it the measure of men is made manifest.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ف أ و

versets.ar: وَأَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَلَا تَنَازَعُوا فَتَفْشَلُوا وَتَذْهَبَ رِيحُكُمْ ۖ وَاصْبِرُوا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-aṭīʿū llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-lā tanāzaʿū fa-tafshalū wa-tadhhaba rīḥukum, wa-ṣbirū, inna llāha maʿa ṣ-ṣābirīn
versets.trad: And obey God and His Messenger, and do not quarrel, lest you lose heart and your strength depart. And be patient: truly God is with the patient.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: concord among the Muslims is the root of religion; and the beginning of corruption and the head of straying is divergence. The wills of the Muslims are unified only because they strip themselves of their force and their power, and all return to God. Patience, he says, is to restrain the soul upon what opposes your inclination — and God is with those who hold to it, by His taking-charge and His sufficiency.

mots.racine: ذ ه ب / ر و ح

versets.ar: وَلَا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا مِن دِيَارِهِم بَطَرًا وَرِئَاءَ النَّاسِ وَيَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā takūnū ka-lladhīna kharajū min diyārihim baṭaran wa-riʾāʾa n-nāsi wa-yaṣuddūna ʿan sabīli llāhi, wa-llāhu bimā yaʿmalūna muḥīṭ
versets.trad: And do not be like those who went forth from their homes in insolence, to be seen by men and to bar from the way of God. And God encompasses all that they do.
themes: going forth without insolence or display
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Meccans went forth from Mecca in the year of Badr to aid the caravan, and pride seized them, and boastfulness settled in them, and the showing-off before men pervaded them — so they fell into the nets of their error and obtained what they had not reckoned. The opposite is in the Prophet's prayer: "do not abandon me to my soul for an instant."

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ن و س

versets.ar: وَإِذْ زَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَعْمَالَهُمْ وَقَالَ لَا غَالِبَ لَكُمُ الْيَوْمَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَإِنِّي جَارٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا تَرَاءَتِ الْفِئَتَانِ نَكَصَ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ وَقَالَ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِّنكُمْ إِنِّي أَرَىٰ مَا لَا تَرَوْنَ إِنِّي أَخَافُ اللَّهَ ۚ وَاللَّهُ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: wa-idh zayyana lahumu sh-shayṭānu aʿmālahum wa-qāla lā ghāliba lakumu l-yawma mina n-nāsi wa-innī jārun lakum, fa-lammā tarāʾati l-fiʾatāni nakaṣa ʿalā ʿaqibayhi wa-qāla innī barīʾun minkum innī arā mā lā tarawna innī akhāfu llāha, wa-llāhu shadīdu l-ʿiqāb
versets.trad: And when Satan made their deeds fair to them and said, "No one shall overcome you today, and I am your protector." But when the two hosts came in sight, he turned upon his heels and said, "I disown you. I see what you do not see. I fear God, and God is severe in punishment."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when Satan, by his whisperings, makes a thing fair to a man, and the soul adorns it for him, the insights of the heedless are blinded to right guidance; the forgetful one remains in the lead of his whisperings, then the assault seizes him from the decree where he does not expect — and neither does Satan keep what he promises, nor does the soul find what it wishes.

mots.racine: ن ك ص / ع ق ب

versets.ar: إِذْ يَقُولُ الْمُنَافِقُونَ وَالَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ غَرَّ هَٰؤُلَاءِ دِينُهُمْ ۗ وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: idh yaqūlu l-munāfiqūna wa-lladhīna fī qulūbihim maraḍun gharra hāʾulāʾi dīnuhum, wa-man yatawakkal ʿalā llāhi fa-inna llāha ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness said, "Their religion has deluded these people." But whoever places his trust in God — truly God is Mighty, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the people of heedlessness and delusion, when the winds of their power blow in the time of their forgetfulness, look upon the people of Reality with an eye of contempt, judge them of feeble state, rank them among the strayed and count them among the ignorant — this is the time of the truce. But the people of certainty and the light of insight remain at peace beneath the unfolding decree, beholding the unseen through a thin veil; the shocks do not unsettle them, the assaults of the moment do not carry them off — soon the star of ease shines forth, and the clouds of difficulty disperse.

mots.def: nifāq: duplicity of heart and tongue.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ تَرَىٰ إِذْ يَتَوَفَّى الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۙ الْمَلَائِكَةُ يَضْرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُمْ وَأَدْبَارَهُمْ وَذُوقُوا عَذَابَ الْحَرِيقِ
versets.tr: wa-law tarā idh yatawaffā lladhīna kafarū l-malāʾikatu yaḍribūna wujūhahum wa-adbārahum wa-dhūqū ʿadhāba l-ḥarīq
versets.trad: If only you could see when the angels take the souls of those who disbelieve, striking their faces and their backs, saying: "Taste the punishment of the burning!"
versets.commentaire: The verse consoles the tried by evoking the swiftness of relief and the imminence of aid. Qushayrī: when the believer sees the requital fall upon those who have sinned, his heart softens toward them; he does not join the procession of gloating, for his heart is empty of the desire for revenge.

mots.def: tawaffī: to cause death by gathering in.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ
versets.tr: dhālika bimā qaddamat aydīkum wa-anna llāha laysa bi-ẓallāmin li-l-ʿabīd
versets.trad: That is for what your hands have sent before, and because God is no wronger of His servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whatever treatment God gives His servants in joy as in affliction, it is from Him beautiful and just — for the kingdom is His kingdom, the creation His creation, the decree His decree.

mots.racine: ق د م / ي د ي
mots.def: taqdīm: to send ahead, to anticipate.

versets.ar: كَدَأْبِ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ ۙ وَالَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَوِيٌّ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: ka-daʾbi āli firʿawna wa-lladhīna min qablihim, kafarū bi-āyāti llāhi fa-akhadhahumu llāhu bi-dhunūbihim, inna llāha qawiyyun shadīdu l-ʿiqāb
versets.trad: Like the way of Pharaoh's folk and those before them: they disbelieved in the signs of God, so God seized them for their sins. Truly God is strong, severe in punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when they walked the path of Pharaoh's folk in straying, We made them walk their path in what We made them taste of punishment and evil state. The law of God does not change in bounty, nor His custom in requital.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَمْ يَكُ مُغَيِّرًا نِّعْمَةً أَنْعَمَهَا عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ حَتَّىٰ يُغَيِّرُوا مَا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ ۙ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-anna llāha lam yaku mughayyiran niʿmatan anʿamahā ʿalā qawmin ḥattā yughayyirū mā bi-anfusihim, wa-anna llāha samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: That is because God would never change a favour He had bestowed upon a people until they change what is in themselves; and because God is Hearing, Knowing.
versets.commentaire: A pivotal verse Qushayrī comments with force: when God gives a favour to a people and wills to prolong it for them, He honours them with the grace of gratitude — and so long as they remain grateful, the favour endures. When He wills to remove a favour, He abases them by abandonment to ingratitude — and as soon as the servant turns from the path of gratitude, the grace is exposed to falling. No gift fades away without the heart having changed first.

mots.racine: غ ي ر / ن ف س
mots.sens: they change what is in themselves

versets.ar: كَدَأْبِ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ ۙ وَالَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَأَغْرَقْنَا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ ۚ وَكُلٌّ كَانُوا ظَالِمِينَ
versets.tr: ka-daʾbi āli firʿawna wa-lladhīna min qablihim, kadhdhabū bi-āyāti rabbihim fa-ahlaknāhum bi-dhunūbihim wa-aghraqnā āla firʿawna, wa-kullun kānū ẓālimīn
versets.trad: Like the way of Pharaoh's folk and those before them: they denied the signs of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins and drowned Pharaoh's folk. And all were wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the varieties of the sins of Pharaoh's folk called down upon them varieties of punishment; likewise these were punished by varieties of punishment, since they had practised varieties of transgression. The use of repeating their mention is to confirm that God never neglects the accountable one, even if He lets him go for a time and an age.

versets.ar: إِنَّ شَرَّ الدَّوَابِّ عِندَ اللَّهِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: inna sharra d-dawābbi ʿinda llāhi lladhīna kafarū fa-hum lā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: The worst of living creatures in the sight of God are those who disbelieve, for they will not believe,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "in the sight of God": in His foreknowledge and His truthful judgment. If they are in His knowledge the worst of creatures, how should they be made happy by the varieties of strivings and the kinds of occurrences? Alas! The Realities do not reverse themselves.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ عَاهَدتَّ مِنْهُمْ ثُمَّ يَنقُضُونَ عَهْدَهُمْ فِي كُلِّ مَرَّةٍ وَهُمْ لَا يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna ʿāhadta minhum thumma yanquḍūna ʿahdahum fī kulli marratin wa-hum lā yattaqūn
versets.trad: those of them with whom you made a pact, then they break their pact every time, and they are not God-fearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are those for whom breaking the pact has become second nature, sparing no effort in ignorance. Now among the capital faults that are not forgiven on this Path is that the servant break a pact or abandon a covenant he has contracted in his heart with God.

versets.ar: فَإِمَّا تَثْقَفَنَّهُمْ فِي الْحَرْبِ فَشَرِّدْ بِهِم مَّنْ خَلْفَهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-immā tathqafannahum fī l-ḥarbi fa-sharrid bihim man khalfahum laʿallahum yadhdhakkarūn
versets.trad: If you come upon them in war, scatter by them those who are behind them, that they may take heed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens it to the spiritual life: he who breaks his pact with God in his heart by returning to the licences of interpretations and the settling into habits — God makes of him an example for those who come after, depriving him of what He had granted him.

mots.def: tashrīd: to drive far in flight.

versets.ar: وَإِمَّا تَخَافَنَّ مِن قَوْمٍ خِيَانَةً فَانبِذْ إِلَيْهِمْ عَلَىٰ سَوَاءٍ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ الْخَائِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-immā takhāfanna min qawmin khiyānatan fa-nbidh ilayhim ʿalā sawāʾin, inna llāha lā yuḥibbu l-khāʾinīn
versets.trad: And if you fear treachery from a people, cast back their pact to them on equal terms. Truly God does not love the treacherous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when you are certain of a people's treachery, declare openly that there is no longer a pact between you and them; when treachery sets in, the mark of faithfulness is extinguished. And each one's treachery is measured by his state: whoever has been miserly with an easy gain has betrayed his pact, deviated from his earnestness — and his punishment is hastened, for God does not love him, and his requital is in humiliation and abasement.

versets.ar: وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا سَبَقُوا ۚ إِنَّهُمْ لَا يُعْجِزُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā yaḥsabanna lladhīna kafarū sabaqū, innahum lā yuʿjizūn
versets.trad: And let not those who disbelieve think they have outstripped. They cannot escape.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, lapidary: how should one whose transformation is in His hands, whose motion is from His power, whose existence and non-existence are from His direction, oppose the Real or contend with Him?

versets.ar: وَأَعِدُّوا لَهُم مَّا اسْتَطَعْتُم مِّن قُوَّةٍ وَمِن رِّبَاطِ الْخَيْلِ تُرْهِبُونَ بِهِ عَدُوَّ اللَّهِ وَعَدُوَّكُمْ وَآخَرِينَ مِن دُونِهِمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَهُمُ اللَّهُ يَعْلَمُهُمْ ۚ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا مِن شَيْءٍ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ يُوَفَّ إِلَيْكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-aʿiddū lahum mā staṭaʿtum min quwwatin wa-min ribāṭi l-khayli turhibūna bihi ʿaduwwa llāhi wa-ʿaduwwakum wa-ākharīna min dūnihim lā taʿlamūnahumu llāhu yaʿlamuhum, wa-mā tunfiqū min shayʾin fī sabīli llāhi yuwaffa ilaykum wa-antum lā tuẓlamūn
versets.trad: And prepare against them whatever force and tethered cavalry you can, to strike terror by it into the enemy of God and your enemy, and others besides them whom you do not know but God knows. And whatever you spend in the way of God will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the forces of the heart: the best of forces is the strength of the heart by God. Men differ in it: one fortifies his heart by the promise of aid; another by the knowledge that the Real knows his state; another by the reality of being contemplated by his Lord; another by preferring the contentment of God to the will of his soul; another by acceptance of what his Master does with him. And the closing allusion: one does not fight in the hope of spoils nor for the satisfying of a grudge — the aim is that the word of God be the highest.

mots.racine: ر ب ط / خ ي ل

versets.ar: وَإِن جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَاجْنَحْ لَهَا وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-in janaḥū li-s-salmi fa-jnaḥ lahā wa-tawakkal ʿalā llāhi, innahu huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīm
versets.trad: And if they incline to peace, then incline to it, and place your trust in God. Truly He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: servanthood is to stand where you have been placed: if you are commanded to fight, do not fall short; if you are commanded to make pact, welcome peace with joy. And place your trust in God in both states — He chooses for you what is better, and directs you to the highest.

versets.ar: وَإِن يُرِيدُوا أَن يَخْدَعُوكَ فَإِنَّ حَسْبَكَ اللَّهُ ۚ هُوَ الَّذِي أَيَّدَكَ بِنَصْرِهِ وَبِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in yurīdū an yakhdaʿūka fa-inna ḥasbaka llāhu, huwa lladhī ayyadaka bi-naṣrihi wa-bi-l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: And if they wish to deceive you, then God is sufficient for you. He it is who strengthened you with His aid and with the believers,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He it is who, by His aid, singled you out; by His gentleness, sustained you; from all evil and all share, purified you; from bondage to things, set you free — and in all states, was yours.

versets.ar: وَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ ۚ لَوْ أَنفَقْتَ مَا فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا مَّا أَلَّفْتَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ أَلَّفَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-allafa bayna qulūbihim, law anfaqta mā fī l-arḍi jamīʿan mā allafta bayna qulūbihim wa-lākinna llāha allafa baynahum, innahu ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: and He united their hearts. Had you spent all that is on earth, you could not have united their hearts, but God united them. Truly He is Mighty, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He united their divided hearts around the religion and the preference for the contentment of the Real. Had this been by the artifices of men, this matter would not have been ordered — not even by every possible effort of deeds and every possible expenditure of wealth. True union is a gift, not a labour.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ حَسْبُكَ اللَّهُ وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu ḥasbuka llāhu wa-mani ttabaʿaka mina l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: O Prophet! God is sufficient for you, and for those of the believers who follow you.
themes: God suffices the Prophet and his followers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is understood that the Prophet's self-subsistence was by God and not by other than God; and all other than God is in need of God's aid, just as the Prophet himself is in need of God's aid.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ حَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَى الْقِتَالِ ۚ إِن يَكُن مِّنكُمْ عِشْرُونَ صَابِرُونَ يَغْلِبُوا مِائَتَيْنِ ۚ وَإِن يَكُن مِّنكُم مِّائَةٌ يَغْلِبُوا أَلْفًا مِّنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu ḥarriḍi l-muʾminīna ʿalā l-qitāl, in yakun minkum ʿishrūna ṣābirūna yaghlibū miʾatayn, wa-in yakun minkum miʾatun yaghlibū alfan mina lladhīna kafarū bi-annahum qawmun lā yafqahūn
versets.trad: O Prophet! Urge the believers to fight. If there are among you twenty steadfast men, they will overcome two hundred; and if there are among you a hundred, they will overcome a thousand of those who disbelieve, for they are a people who do not understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the believer does not increase in weakness in his soul without increasing in strength in his heart — for self-reliance through the soul's force is the fruit of heedlessness, and the strength of the heart by God is, in truth, what is real.

versets.ar: الْآنَ خَفَّفَ اللَّهُ عَنكُمْ وَعَلِمَ أَنَّ فِيكُمْ ضَعْفًا ۚ فَإِن يَكُن مِّنكُم مِّائَةٌ صَابِرَةٌ يَغْلِبُوا مِائَتَيْنِ ۚ وَإِن يَكُن مِّنكُمْ أَلْفٌ يَغْلِبُوا أَلْفَيْنِ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: al-āna khaffafa llāhu ʿankum wa-ʿalima anna fīkum ḍaʿfan, fa-in yakun minkum miʾatun ṣābiratun yaghlibū miʾatayn, wa-in yakun minkum alfun yaghlibū alfayni bi-idhni llāhi, wa-llāhu maʿa ṣ-ṣābirīn
versets.trad: Now God has lightened your burden, for He knows there is weakness in you. So if there are among you a hundred steadfast, they will overcome two hundred; and if there are a thousand, they will overcome two thousand by the leave of God. And God is with the steadfast.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the weakness He knew in them was the weakness of bodies, not of hearts: so He lightened for them the obligation to fight, by the excuse mentioned in the Book. The common bear hardships with their souls and their bodies; the elect, with their hearts and their aspirations — and it has been said: the heart bears what the body does not bear.

versets.ar: مَا كَانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَن يَكُونَ لَهُ أَسْرَىٰ حَتَّىٰ يُثْخِنَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ تُرِيدُونَ عَرَضَ الدُّنْيَا وَاللَّهُ يُرِيدُ الْآخِرَةَ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: mā kāna li-nabiyyin an yakūna lahu asrā ḥattā yuthkhina fī l-arḍi, turīdūna ʿaraḍa d-dunyā wa-llāhu yurīdu l-ākhirata, wa-llāhu ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: It is not for a prophet to have captives until he has thoroughly subdued the land. You desire the fleeting goods of this world, but God desires the Hereafter. And God is Mighty, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sanctity of the Law is contrary to the mercy of nature; the condition of servanthood is that the servant prefer God, and when the command is to rigour, it is as He said: "let no pity for them take hold of you in the religion of God".

mots.def: ithkhān: to press the combat to the full.

mots.racine: ع ر ض / د ن و
mots.sens: the fleeting good of this world

versets.ar: لَّوْلَا كِتَابٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ سَبَقَ لَمَسَّكُمْ فِيمَا أَخَذْتُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: lawlā kitābun mina llāhi sabaqa la-massakum fīmā akhadhtum ʿadhābun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: Had there not been a prior decree from God, a great punishment would have touched you for what you took.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had God not decreed in His eternity that the spoils would be lawful for Muḥammad and his community, a great punishment would have touched you for the ransom you took from the captives of Badr — but God made the spoils lawful for you, and removed the penalty from you.

versets.ar: فَكُلُوا مِمَّا غَنِمْتُمْ حَلَالًا طَيِّبًا ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-kulū mimmā ghanimtum ḥalālan ṭayyiban, wa-ttaqū llāha, inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: So eat of what you have taken as spoils, lawful and good. And fear God: truly God is Forgiving, Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds three measures of the lawful: the lawful is what is permitted; the lawful-good is to know that it comes from God as a grace, and not from you as an acquired right. The pure lawful is what does not make its owner forget the Worshipped within him. It is that whose owner, in taking it, is not absent from the face of his Lord.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُل لِّمَن فِي أَيْدِيكُم مِّنَ الْأَسْرَىٰ إِن يَعْلَمِ اللَّهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ خَيْرًا يُؤْتِكُمْ خَيْرًا مِّمَّا أُخِذَ مِنكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu qul li-man fī aydīkum mina l-asrā in yaʿlami llāhu fī qulūbikum khayran yuʾtikum khayran mimmā ukhidha minkum wa-yaghfir lakum, wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: O Prophet! Say to the captives in your hands: "If God knows any good in your hearts, He will give you better than what was taken from you, and He will forgive you. And God is Forgiving, Merciful."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what they will be given is better than what was taken from them. This may mean the beautiful reward of the hereafter, or the beautiful recompense of this world; and it is also that to which He brings them: the grace of obedience and the sweetness of faith — better than what was taken from them. It is, too, the contentment He gives them for the poverty they are in, after they were rich in association.

versets.ar: وَإِن يُرِيدُوا خِيَانَتَكَ فَقَدْ خَانُوا اللَّهَ مِن قَبْلُ فَأَمْكَنَ مِنْهُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-in yurīdū khiyānataka fa-qad khānū llāha min qablu fa-amkana minhum, wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīm
versets.trad: But if they wish to betray you, they have betrayed God before, and He gave you power over them. And God is Knowing, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if they return to fight you after you have shown them grace by freeing them, and break your pact — for treachery is their habit and their way — We shall give you power over them a second time, as We gave you power over their capture the first.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهَاجَرُوا وَجَاهَدُوا بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَالَّذِينَ آوَوا وَّنَصَرُوا أُولَٰئِكَ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يُهَاجِرُوا مَا لَكُم مِّن وَلَايَتِهِم مِّن شَيْءٍ حَتَّىٰ يُهَاجِرُوا ۚ وَإِنِ اسْتَنصَرُوكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ فَعَلَيْكُمُ النَّصْرُ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُم مِّيثَاقٌ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-hājarū wa-jāhadū bi-amwālihim wa-anfusihim fī sabīli llāhi wa-lladhīna āwaw wa-naṣarū ulāʾika baʿḍuhum awliyāʾu baʿḍin, wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-lam yuhājirū mā lakum min walāyatihim min shayʾin ḥattā yuhājirū, wa-ini stanṣarūkum fī d-dīni fa-ʿalaykumu n-naṣru illā ʿalā qawmin baynakum wa-baynahum mīthāqun, wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna baṣīr
versets.trad: Those who believed, emigrated, and strove with their wealth and their lives in the way of God, and those who gave shelter and aid — they are allies of one another. As for those who believed but did not emigrate, you owe them no alliance until they emigrate. Yet if they seek your aid in religion, then it is your duty to aid them, except against a people with whom you have a treaty. And God sees well what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens here the inner hijra: the perfection of the emigration is the abandonment of blameworthy traits, and the banishing of the soul by refusing to answer what it calls to of its passions; thence, the banishing of evil companions, the distancing from the places where the servant committed his fault, then the emigration from the dwellings of the shares to the dwellings of the contentment of the Real. And the Anṣār are those who prefer their brothers to themselves, even in their own need — their common in the things of this world, their elite in the gifts of the other Abode, and the elite of the elite in all that is confirmed of the noble states up to what thought cannot conceive.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ إِلَّا تَفْعَلُوهُ تَكُن فِتْنَةٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَفَسَادٌ كَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū baʿḍuhum awliyāʾu baʿḍin, illā tafʿalūhu takun fitnatun fī l-arḍi wa-fasādun kabīr
versets.trad: And those who disbelieve are allies of one another. Unless you do this, there will be sedition on earth and great corruption.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He cut the bond between them and the believers: the believer keeps the strangers at a distance and draws the near ones close. As for the disbelievers, they are allies of one another — as it is said: "the bird of the sky alights upon those of its own kind."

mots.racine: ب ع ض / و ل ي
ف س د / ك ب ر

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهَاجَرُوا وَجَاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَالَّذِينَ آوَوا وَّنَصَرُوا أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ۚ لَّهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-hājarū wa-jāhadū fī sabīli llāhi wa-lladhīna āwaw wa-naṣarū ulāʾika humu l-muʾminūna ḥaqqan, lahum maghfiratun wa-rizqun karīm
versets.trad: And those who believed, emigrated, and strove in the way of God, and those who gave shelter and aid — they are the true believers. For them is forgiveness and a generous provision.
versets.commentaire: The sūra closes upon the same formula as its opening (v. 4), like a completed circle: faith, hijra, struggle — there is the triad of the truth. Qushayrī: these are the true believers, who have verified the true by the true and attested the sincere by the sincere; for them forgiveness as enduring veiling, and noble provision as a gift without reckoning.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ح ق ق
ر ز ق / ك ر م

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِن بَعْدُ وَهَاجَرُوا وَجَاهَدُوا مَعَكُمْ فَأُولَٰئِكَ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَأُولُو الْأَرْحَامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلَىٰ بِبَعْضٍ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū min baʿdu wa-hājarū wa-jāhadū maʿakum fa-ulāʾika minkum, wa-ulū l-arḥāmi baʿḍuhum awlā bi-baʿḍin fī kitābi llāhi, inna llāha bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīm
versets.trad: And those who believed afterward, and emigrated, and strove alongside you — they are of you. Yet those bound by ties of blood are nearer to one another in the Book of God. Truly God is Knowing of all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī closes upon an open promise: He intends those who followed their way in the present state, and those who will join them in the future and the states to come: affection gathers them, walāya envelops them, and they have with God, in the other Abode, abundance of reward and deliverance from punishment. In this abode: walāya and mutual aid, love and nearness — and God knows best.

mots.racine: أ و ل / ر ح م
mots.sens: those of the ties of blood
